title: melissa officinalis subsp. officinalis
intro: Melissa officinalis subsp. officinalis, also known as Lemon Balm, is a subspecies of the Melissa officinalis plant, a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is valued for its soothing and calming properties, which have been used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Lemon Balm include reducing stress and promoting relaxation, improving mood and cognitive function, and alleviating symptoms of menopause and irritable bowel syndrome. Lemon Balm can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or oil, and is often used in herbal remedies and culinary applications, such as flavoring desserts and drinks.
